summary: klaudius ships hardcoded Telegram Bot API exfiltration channel
details: The package's bundled output (dist/chunk-SZ4KCTSL.js line 6 and dist/bin.js line 11) contains hardcoded fetch/POST calls to https://api.telegram.org, the Telegram Bot API endpoint used as a covert C2/exfil channel. Telegram Bot API is a documented attacker pattern for siphoning data out of victim machines via a bot-token URL embedded in the package; the host is universally reachable and resists IP/domain blocking. The CLI entry point (dist/bin.js) invokes this code path, so installing klaudius and running its CLI causes outbound POSTs to an attacker-controlled Telegram bot. There is no legitimate reason for a developer-tool npm package to hardcode a Telegram bot endpoint in its bundled CLI.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
